title: Coverage gate + verification gate (dual "exhausted?"; default fail-closed; sole conclusion arbiter)
context: The 'no premature conclusion' refusal is enforced by a dual gate: only when both the coverage gate and the verification gate report the corpus exhausted may a final answer be emitted - which, across all domains, is effectively never. This safety-critical M1 task implements that dual gate as the sole conclusion arbiter, defaulting fail-closed to 'not exhausted' so the failure mode is over-researching, never over-concluding.
objective: Implement the dual coverage + verification gate as the sole path to a final answer: the coverage gate reports exhausted only with no open frontier nodes and no new sources across K consecutive cycles; the verification gate reports exhausted only with all claims verified, no unresolved contradictions, and reproducibility >= threshold; the gate defaults fail-closed to 'not exhausted'.
acceptanceCriteria: Coverage gate reports 'exhausted' only if there are no open frontier nodes AND no new sources discovered across K consecutive cycles (K parameterized + governance-set)
Verification gate reports 'exhausted' only if all claims verified, no unresolved contradictions, and reproducibility >= threshold
The gate is the only path to a final answer, defaults fail-closed to 'not exhausted', and treats any missing data / gate error / ambiguity as 'not exhausted' (no final answer)
Reviewed and signed off by the gate reviewer + maintainer (recorded)
